points.text: ASCO 2026 conference highlighted bispecific antibodies as a major therapeutic class alongside ADCs, with specific data presented on daraxanrasib and ivonescimab in oncology indications.
Immatics — IMA402 — PRAME bispecific — Identified as part of the company's broad PRAME franchise alongside cell therapies and combination strategies with immune checkpoint inhibitors.
CytomX Therapeutics and Regeneron expanded their strategic research collaboration to advance conditional bispecific therapeutics for cancer treatment.
Dark Horse Consulting Group and Altruist Biologics signed an MOU to support the development of bispecific antibodies and other biologics in China via integrated CMC and manufacturing services.

Crescent Biopharma — PD-1 x VEGF bispecific antibody — Clinical-stage pipeline — Lead program identified as bispecific antibody targeting PD-1 and VEGF for solid tumors; novel ADCs also in development.
Immatics presented clinical data on IMA401, a MAGEA4/8 TCR bispecific, at the 2026 ASCO Annual Meeting with simultaneous publication in Nature Medicine.
Immatics reported that IMA401 MAGEA4/8 TCR bispecific achieved a 29% confirmed ORR and 64% DCR in head and neck cancer at the recommended Phase 2 dose.
Immatics is advancing a combination strategy of IMA401 (MAGEA4/8 bispecific) and IMA402 (PRAME bispecific) in lung cancer with first data expected in 2027.
ASCO 2026 featured a comparative analysis of bispecific antibodies versus antibody-drug conjugates (ADCs) alongside a 'RAS' revolution and advancements in prostate cancer therapeutics.

Dark Horse Consulting Group and Altruist Biologics (Innovent Biologics subsidiary) signed an MOU to advance biologics manufacturing in China, explicitly including development and production of bispecific antibodies alongside ADCs and monoclonal antibodies.

Akeso and Summit Therapeutics — ivonescimab — Squamous non-small cell lung cancer — Phase 3 trial data presented at ASCO 2026 showed 34% reduction in risk of death vs standard of care; drug described as 'two drugs in one' bispecific antibody.
BioNTech and Pfizer — Multiple bispecific programs — Oncology indications — Presented clinical data on bispecific antibody candidates at ASCO 2026 meeting.

Retrospective analysis presented at ASCO 2026 compared BCMA CAR T-cell therapy against teclistamab (bispecific antibody) in multiple myeloma, showing superior 2-year overall survival (HR 0.61) but higher immune-related toxicities.

I-SPY 2.2 Phase 2 trial results presented at ASCO 2026 demonstrated a 53% pathologic complete response rate in immune-positive, HER2-negative breast cancer patients treated with the PD-1/TIGIT bispecific antibody rilvegostomig combined with T-DXd.

Izalontamab brengitecan (iza-bren), a bispecific antibody-drug conjugate targeting EGFR and HER3, demonstrated a 71% reduction in risk of disease progression or death (HR 0.29) in Phase 3 PANKU-Breast02 trial for pretreated metastatic triple-negative breast cancer.

Ivonescimab, the world's first PD-1/VEGF dual-target bispecific antibody, achieved a statistically significant overall survival benefit (median 27.89 vs 23.69 months; HR 0.66) over tislelizumab plus chemotherapy in Phase 3 HARMONi-6 trial for advanced squamous NSCLC.

The Phase 3 PANKU-Breast02 trial met dual primary endpoints with izalontamab brengitecan showing median PFS of 8.5 months versus 3.1 months for chemotherapy in 418 patients with unresected locally advanced or metastatic TNBC.
Ivonescimab plus chemotherapy showed consistent OS benefit across PD-L1 subgroups in squamous NSCLC, with hazard ratios of 0.64 for TPS <1% and 0.68 for TPS ≥1% in the HARMONi-6 trial.

Janssen's teclistamab (Tecvayli) and talquetamab (Talvey) bispecific antibodies demonstrated feasibility for community-based outpatient administration when combined with prophylactic tocilizumab, eliminating grade 3+ CRS or ICANS events in the phase 2 OPTec/OPTal trial.

Teclistamab monotherapy achieved a 71% reduction in risk of progression or death (HR 0.29) and 84.5% overall response rate in relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma patients in the phase 3 MajesTEC-9 trial, outperforming investigator's choice regimens.

Prophylactic tocilizumab reduced cytokine release syndrome (CRS) incidence to approximately 12.2% in patients receiving bispecific antibodies teclistamab or talquetamab, with no grade 3 or higher CRS or ICANS events observed across trial arms.

Akeso and Summit Therapeutics — ivonescimab — Lung Cancer — PD-1/VEGF bispecific reduced risk of death by 34% vs standard chemotherapy plus PD-1 inhibitor in Phase 3 trial; deal activity surged following 2024 results.
BioNTech and Pfizer presented bispecific antibody data at the 2026 ASCO Annual Meeting, highlighting clinical progress in their oncology pipeline.

A retrospective cohort study presented at ASCO 2026 compared BCMA CAR T-cell therapy against the bispecific antibody teclistamab (Tecvayli) in multiple myeloma, noting the need for prospective studies to define optimal sequencing between these modalities.

I-SPY 2.2 trial presented at ASCO 2026 showed rilvegostomig (dual PD-1/TIGIT bispecific antibody) plus fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan-nxki achieved 53% pathologic complete response in immune-positive, HER2-negative breast cancer subgroups.

PANKU-Breast02 phase 3 trial demonstrated izalontamab brengitecan (bispecific ADC targeting EGFR/HER3) reduced risk of disease progression or death by 71% (HR 0.29) and death by 40% (HR 0.60) in metastatic triple-negative breast cancer.
Rilvegostomig combination therapy in I-SPY 2.2 enabled treatment de-escalation strategies, with 57% pCR in HR-positive/immune-positive and 52% pCR in HR-negative/immune-positive patients.
Izalontamab brengitecan achieved median PFS of 8.5 months versus 3.1 months with chemotherapy in pretreated metastatic TNBC, with 6-month PFS probabilities of 62.0% vs 17.6%.

Akeso's ivonescimab, the world's first PD-1/VEGF dual-target bispecific antibody, demonstrated a statistically significant overall survival benefit in the phase 3 HARMONi-6 trial for advanced squamous NSCLC (median OS 27.89 vs 23.69 months; HR 0.66; p=0.0017) versus tislelizumab plus chemotherapy.

Prophylactic tocilizumab enabled outpatient administration of bispecific antibodies teclistamab and talquetamab in relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma by reducing cytokine release syndrome (CRS) to grade 1/2 only, with no grade 3+ CRS or ICANS events reported in the phase 2 OPTec/OPTal trial.

Janssen Biotech — Teclistamab (Tecvayli) — Relapsed/Refractory Multiple Myeloma — Phase 3 MajesTEC-9 trial: HR 0.29 for PFS (71% risk reduction), median PFS not reached vs 8.2 months; HR 0.60 for OS; ORR 84.5% vs 54.2%; CR+ rate 65.9% vs 16.8%.

Akeso / Summit Therapeutics — Ivonescimab — Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer — PD-1/VEGF bispecific antibody; Phase 3 trial data showed 34% reduction in risk of death vs standard chemotherapy plus PD-1 inhibitor.

Astellas / Amgen — Daraxanrasib — Non-small cell lung cancer — Presented at ASCO 2026 as a bispecific antibody targeting EGFR and MET; data showed efficacy in patients with EGFR mutations and MET amplification.

Akeso / BeiGene — Ivonescimab — Non-small cell lung cancer — Presented at ASCO 2026 as a PD-1/VEGF bispecific antibody; data demonstrated improved progression-free survival in combination with chemotherapy.

Immatics — IMA402 — PRAME-positive solid tumors — Bispecific T-cell engager (BiTE) candidate included in the company's PRAME franchise pipeline for monotherapy and combination with IMA401 MAGEA4/8 bispecific.
CytomX Therapeutics — Regeneron Collaboration — Conditional Bispecific Therapeutics — Strategic research partnership expanded to advance novel bispecific candidates for oncology indications.

Dark Horse Consulting Group / Altruist Biologics — Bispecific Antibody Development — China Manufacturing — MOU signed to provide end-to-end cGMP production and regulatory strategy for bispecifics and ADCs.
Crescent Biopharma — PD-1 x VEGF bispecific antibody — Solid tumors — Clinical-stage bispecific antibody program advancing as single agent and in combination regimens.

Immatics — IMA401 — MAGEA4/8-positive solid tumors (Head and Neck, Lung) — Phase 1 data presented at ASCO 2026: 29% ORR and 64% DCR in head and neck cancer; combination with IMA402 (PRAME bispecific) now enrolling.
ASCO 2026 — Bispecific antibodies — Oncology — Presented as a major therapeutic modality competing with ADCs and driving a 'RAS' revolution in cancer treatment strategies.
Immatics — IMA402 — PRAME-positive solid tumors — Bispecific T-cell engager evaluated as monotherapy and in combination with immune checkpoint inhibitors or IMA401 MAGEA4/8 bispecific.

Dark Horse Consulting Group / Altruist Biologics — Bispecific Antibodies — China Manufacturing — MOU established to provide end-to-end cGMP production and regulatory strategy for bispecifics via Altruist's 232,000L capacity in Suzhou and Hangzhou.
Crescent Biopharma — PD-1 x VEGF bispecific antibody — Solid tumors — Clinical-stage lead program described as a bispecific antibody targeting PD-1 and VEGF.

Akeso / Summit Therapeutics — ivonescimab — Squamous non-small cell lung cancer — Phase 3 trial met primary endpoint with 34% reduction in risk of death vs standard of care; data presented at ASCO 2026.
BioNTech / Pfizer — Bispecific antibody candidates — Oncology — Presented clinical data on bispecific programs at ASCO 2026 annual meeting.

Jacobi Medical Center / Albert Einstein College of Medicine — Teclistamab (bispecific) vs BCMA CAR T — Multiple Myeloma — Retrospective study showed 2-year OS HR 0.61 (95% CI 0.43-0.87; P=.005) favoring CAR T, but CRS incidence HR 1.48 (P=.003) and ICANS HR 2.02 (P=.007) higher with CAR T.

Mayo Clinic / I-SPY 2.2 — Rilvegostomig (PD-1/TIGIT bispecific) + T-DXd — HER2-negative Breast Cancer — Phase 2 neoadjuvant trial achieved 53% estimated pCR in immune-positive subgroups, exceeding predefined efficacy thresholds.

Izalontamab brengitecan — EGFR/HER3 bispecific ADC — Metastatic Triple-Negative Breast Cancer — Phase 3 PANKU-Breast02 met dual primary endpoints: median PFS 8.5 vs 3.1 months (HR 0.29, p<0.0001); median OS 15.9 vs 12.5 months (HR 0.60, p=0.0019).

Ivonescimab — PD-1/VEGF bispecific antibody — Advanced Squamous NSCLC — Phase 3 HARMONi-6 interim OS analysis: median OS 27.89 vs 23.69 months (HR 0.66, p=0.0017) vs tislelizumab plus chemotherapy; 12-month OS rate 78.9% vs 72.2%.
Janssen — Teclistamab (Tecvayli) — Relapsed/Refractory Multiple Myeloma — Phase 3 MajesTEC-9: Median PFS not reached vs 8.2 months (HR 0.29); 18-month PFS 69.8% vs 26.9%; ORR 84.5% vs 54.2%.

Janssen — Talquetamab (Talvey) + Teclistamab — Relapsed/Refractory Multiple Myeloma — Phase 2 OPTec/OPTal: Outpatient administration supported by prophylactic tocilizumab; ORR 71.4% (talquetamab) and 68.9% (teclistamab); 0% grade 3+ CRS/ICANS.
Janssen — Teclistamab — Relapsed/Refractory Multiple Myeloma — Phase 3 MajesTEC-9: 18-month OS rate 79.2% vs 68.6% (HR 0.60); CR or better rate 65.9% vs 16.8% vs investigator's choice (PVd/Kd).

Akeso / Summit Therapeutics — ivonescimab — Lung Cancer — PD-1/VEGF bispecific demonstrated 34% mortality reduction in Phase 3 trial; industry focus shifting toward liver cancer indications per analyst commentary.
BioNTech / Pfizer — Bispecific Antibodies — Oncology — Presented clinical data at ASCO 2026 demonstrating advancement in bispecific pipeline programs.

Jacobi Medical Center / Albert Einstein College of Medicine — Teclistamab (bispecific) vs BCMA CAR T — Multiple Myeloma — Retrospective analysis showed CAR T improved 2-year OS (HR 0.61, p=0.005) vs teclistamab but with higher immune-related toxicities (CRS HR 1.48, ICANS HR 2.02).
I-SPY 2.2 Trial — Rilvegostomig + T-DXd — HER2-negative breast cancer — Phase 2 adaptive trial met efficacy thresholds with 53% pCR in immune-positive subgroups; presented at ASCO 2026.

PANKU-Breast02 Trial — Izalontamab brengitecan — Metastatic triple-negative breast cancer — Phase 3 met dual primary endpoints: median PFS 8.5 vs 3.1 months (HR 0.29); median OS 15.9 vs 12.5 months (HR 0.60).

Akeso — Ivonescimab — Advanced squamous NSCLC — Phase 3 HARMONi-6 interim OS analysis: median OS 27.89 months vs 23.69 months (tislelizumab control); HR 0.66 (95% CI 0.50–0.87); p=0.0017; 12-month OS rate 78.9% vs 72.2%.

Janssen / Adicet Bio — Teclistamab + Talquetamab — Relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma — Phase 2 OPTec/OPTal trial with prophylactic tocilizumab: 0% grade 3+ CRS or ICANS; ORR 68.9% (teclistamab) and 71.4% (talquetamab); supports community-based outpatient dosing.

Janssen Biotech — Teclistamab (Tecvayli) — Relapsed/Refractory Multiple Myeloma — Phase 3 MajesTEC-9 met primary endpoint: 71% reduction in progression/death risk (HR 0.29, p<0.0001) vs investigator's choice; 18-month PFS 69.8% vs 26.9%.

Akeso / Summit Therapeutics — Ivonescimab — Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer — PD-1/VEGF bispecific; Phase 3 data presented showing 34% mortality risk reduction vs chemo + PD-1 inhibitor, driving billions in industry investment.
Astellas — Bispecific antibody commercialization strategy — Daraxanrasib (EGFR/MET bispecific) — Advancing Phase 3 development following positive ASCO 2026 data in NSCLC.
Akeso — Global expansion of bispecific portfolio — Ivonescimab (PD-1/VEGF bispecific) — Generating pivotal data at ASCO 2026 to support regulatory filings in NSCLC.

Immatics — Precision targeting of PRAME — IMA402 (PRAME bispecific) + IMA401 (MAGEA4/8 bispecific) — Global leader in PRAME modalities with three product candidates and two therapeutic modalities including bispecifics.
CytomX Therapeutics — Conditional bispecific therapeutic development — Pipeline includes PROBODY platform candidates — Strategic collaboration with Regeneron expanded for cancer treatment.

Altruist Biologics (Innovent Biologics subsidiary) — Bispecific antibody manufacturing — China cGMP facilities (232,000L total capacity) — Partnered with Dark Horse Consulting Group for regulatory and CMC support.
Crescent Biopharma — Oncology pipeline expansion — PD-1 x VEGF bispecific antibody + ADCs — Clinical-stage company building world-leading oncology portfolio with bispecific and ADC modalities.
Immatics — Precision targeting via TCR bispecifics — IMA401 (MAGEA4/8 bispecific) + IMA402 (PRAME bispecific) — Phase 1 data supports combination strategy in lung cancer; first data expected 2027.

Immatics — Precision targeting of PRAME with bispecifics and cell therapies — IMA402 (PRAME bispecific) + IMA401 (MAGEA4/8 bispecific) + IMA203CD8 (TCR T-cell) — Global leader in PRAME franchise with multiple clinical-stage programs.

Altruist Biologics — Bispecific antibody CDMO services — Innovent Biologics subsidiary — Operating 232,000L total capacity in China with explicit capability for bispecific antibody development and commercial manufacturing.
Crescent Biopharma — Bispecific antibody development — PD-1 x VEGF bispecific — Clinical-stage pipeline includes lead bispecific program alongside novel ADCs.

Immatics N.V. — Precision targeting of PRAME and MAGEA4/8 via bispecifics and cell therapies — IMA401 (MAGEA4/8 TCR bispecific) + IMA402 (PRAME bispecific) — Phase 1 data presented at ASCO 2026; combination strategy advancing in lung cancer with 2027 data readout planned.

Akeso — Commercialization of ivonescimab — ivonescimab (PD-1/VEGF bispecific) — Phase 3 data in squamous NSCLC presented at ASCO 2026; developed in China, partnered with Summit Therapeutics for global rights.
BioNTech / Pfizer — Bispecific antibody pipeline — Multiple oncology bispecifics — Presented clinical data at ASCO 2026; advancing bispecific programs in cancer indications.

Janssen (implied via Teclistamab/Tecvayli) — Bispecific antibody commercialization in multiple myeloma — Teclistamab — Used as comparator in ASCO 2026 retrospective study showing inferior OS vs CAR T but lower immune-mediated toxicity profile.

Genmab / Regeneron (implied via Rilvegostomig) — Bispecific antibody development in solid tumors — Rilvegostomig — Phase 2 I-SPY 2.2 trial data presented at ASCO 2026 showing 57% pCR in HR-positive/immune-positive and 52% in HR-negative/immune-positive subgroups.
Akeso (implied via ivonescimab) — Commercialization of PD-1/VEGF bispecific antibody — Ivonescimab — Approved in China for 2 lung cancer indications since 2024; advancing Phase 3 HARMONi-6 globally.

Fudan University Cancer Institute / Investigator Jiong Wu — Development of EGFR/HER3 bispecific ADC — Izalontamab brengitecan — Phase 3 PANKU-Breast02 results presented at ASCO 2026 supporting new standard of care in pretreated metastatic TNBC.

Janssen — Commercialization of bispecific antibody portfolio in multiple myeloma — Teclistamab (Tecvayli) and Talquetamab (Talvey) — Phase 3 MajesTEC-9 and Phase 2 OPTec/OPTal trials confirm outpatient feasibility and superior PFS/OS vs standard of care.

Akeso — PD-1/VEGF bispecific commercialization — ivonescimab — Licensed to Summit Therapeutics; Phase 3 data supports 34% mortality reduction in lung cancer; strategic pivot to liver cancer indications under evaluation.

Summit Therapeutics — PD-1/VEGF bispecific expansion — ivonescimab — Licensed from Akeso; Phase 3 trial met primary endpoint with 34% mortality reduction; exploring liver cancer indications beyond lung cancer.
BioNTech / Pfizer — Bispecific antibody development — Oncology pipeline — Active clinical presentation of bispecific data at ASCO 2026.

Janssen (Teclistamab/TECVAYLI) — Bispecific antibody commercialization — Teclistamab (BCMA x CD3) — Used as comparator in ASCO 2026 retrospective study showing inferior 2-year OS (HR 0.61) vs BCMA CAR T in multiple myeloma.

Regeneron Pharmaceuticals — Advancing rilvegostomig (PD-1/TIGIT bispecific) — Rilvegostomig + T-DXd combination — Phase 2 I-SPY 2.2 data supports neoadjuvant de-escalation in HER2-negative breast cancer.
AstraZeneca / Daiichi Sankyo — Developing izalontamab brengitecan (EGFR/HER3 bispecific ADC) — Iza-bren — Phase 3 PANKU-Breast02 results support new standard of care in pretreated metastatic TNBC.

Akeso — Commercialization of first-in-class bispecific platform — Ivonescimab (PD-1/VEGF bispecific) — Approved in China for 2 lung cancer indications since 2024; Phase 3 HARMONi-6 met primary OS endpoint in squamous NSCLC.

Janssen / Adicet Bio — Outpatient bispecific antibody deployment — Teclistamab (Tecvayli) and Talquetamab (Talvey) — Phase 2 OPTec/OPTal data supports prophylactic tocilizumab strategy to eliminate grade 3+ CRS/ICANS in community settings.
Janssen Biotech — Advancing bispecific antibody leadership in myeloma — Teclistamab (Tecvayli) — Phase 3 MajesTEC-9 confirmed superior PFS and OS vs standard care; deep responses with 65.9% CR+ rate.
Summit Therapeutics — Commercializing PD-1/VEGF bispecific platform — Ivonescimab (licensed from Akeso) — Phase 3 lung cancer data validated 34% mortality benefit; triggered wave of industry deals.

Daraxanrasib — Mechanism: Bispecific antibody targeting EGFR and MET — Phase: 3 — Population: NSCLC with EGFR mutations and MET amplification — Endpoints/Results: Presented at ASCO 2026 showing clinical activity in resistant disease.

Ivonescimab — Mechanism: Bispecific antibody targeting PD-1 and VEGF — Phase: 3 — Population: NSCLC — Endpoints/Results: Presented at ASCO 2026 demonstrating improved progression-free survival vs standard of care.

IMA402 — Mechanism: PRAME-directed bispecific T-cell engager — Phase: Clinical-stage (implied by franchise status) — Population: PRAME-positive solid tumors — Context: Evaluated as monotherapy and in combination with immune checkpoint inhibitors.

Conditional Bispecific Therapeutics — Mechanism: Tumor microenvironment-localized activation — Phase: Preclinical/Early Clinical (Collaboration focus) — Population: Cancer patients — Status: Joint development by CytomX and Regeneron.
PD-1 x VEGF bispecific antibody — Mechanism: Dual targeting of PD-1 and VEGF — Phase: Clinical-stage — Population: Solid tumors — Strategy: Single agent and combination regimens.

IMA401 — Mechanism: TCR bispecific targeting MAGEA4/8 — Phase: 1 — Population: Heavily pretreated solid tumors (Head and Neck, Lung) — Comparator: None specified (dose-finding) — Endpoints/Results: 29% confirmed ORR, 64% DCR, 8.8 months mDOR in head and neck cancer at RP2D; favorable tolerability with or without pembrolizumab.

Bispecific antibodies — Mechanism: Dual-target engagement (implied by class) — Phase: Not specified in abstract — Population: Oncology patients — Comparator: Antibody-drug conjugates (ADCs) — Context: Highlighted at ASCO 2026 as a key modality driving a 'RAS' revolution and prostate cancer step change.

IMA402 — Mechanism: PRAME-directed bispecific T-cell engager — Phase: Clinical-stage (implied by franchise status) — Population: PRAME-positive solid tumors — Comparator: Not specified — Status: Evaluated as monotherapy and in combination with immune checkpoint inhibitors or IMA401.

ivonescimab — Mechanism: PD-1/VEGF bispecific antibody — Phase: 3 — Population: Squamous non-small cell lung cancer — Comparator: Standard of care — Results: 34% reduction in risk of death; data presented at ASCO 2026.

Rilvegostomig — Mechanism: Dual PD-1/TIGIT bispecific antibody blockade — Phase: 2 (I-SPY 2.2) — Population: Early-stage, high-risk, HER2-negative breast cancer (immune-positive subtypes) — Comparator: Historical/Control arms — Endpoints: 53% estimated pCR rate in immune-positive subgroup; 57% in HR+/immune-positive; 52% in HR-/immune-positive.

Teclistamab — Mechanism: BCMAxCD3 bispecific antibody — Phase: Approved/Real-world comparison — Population: Relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma — Comparator: BCMA CAR T-cell therapy — Endpoints: 2-year OS HR 0.61 vs CAR T; lower incidence of CRS (HR 1.48 vs CAR T) and ICANS (HR 2.02 vs CAR T).

Izalontamab brengitecan — Mechanism: Bispecific ADC targeting EGFR (high affinity) and HER3 (low affinity) with topoisomerase I inhibitor payload — Phase: 3 — Population: Pretreated metastatic TNBC (1-2 prior lines) — Comparator: Chemotherapy (eribulin, capecitabine, gemcitabine, vinorelbine) — Results: 71% risk reduction in progression/death; 6-month PFS 62.0% vs 17.6%.

Ivonescimab — Mechanism: PD-1/VEGF dual-target bispecific antibody — Phase: 3 (HARMONi-6) — Population: Previously untreated advanced squamous NSCLC (stage IIIB-IV, no EGFR/ALK) — Comparator: Tislelizumab plus chemotherapy — Results: Median OS 27.89 vs 23.69 months (HR 0.66); 24-month OS rate 64.7% vs 48.6%.

Teclistamab — Mechanism: BCMAxCD3 bispecific T-cell engager — Phase: 3 (MajesTEC-9) — Population: RRMM (1-3 prior lines) — Comparator: PVd or Kd — Endpoints: PFS HR 0.29 (P<0.0001); OS HR 0.60 (P=0.0020); ORR 84.5%.

Talquetamab — Mechanism: GPRC5DxCD3 bispecific T-cell engager — Phase: 2 (OPTec/OPTal) — Population: RRMM — Comparator: None (single arm with tocilizumab) — Endpoints: ORR 71.4%; 0% grade 3+ CRS/ICANS with prophylactic tocilizumab.

Teclistamab — Mechanism: BCMAxCD3 bispecific T-cell engager — Phase: 2 (OPTec/OPTal) — Population: RRMM — Comparator: None (single arm with tocilizumab) — Endpoints: ORR 68.9%; 0% grade 3+ CRS/ICANS with prophylactic tocilizumab.

ivonescimab — Mechanism: PD-1/VEGF bispecific antibody — Phase: 3 — Population: Lung cancer patients — Comparator: Standard chemotherapy plus PD-1 inhibitor — Results: 34% reduction in risk of death; potential efficacy in liver cancer under investigation.

Teclistamab — Mechanism: BCMA x CD3 bispecific T-cell engager — Phase: Approved/Real-world comparison — Population: Relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma — Comparator: BCMA CAR T-cell therapy — Results: Inferior 2-year overall survival (HR 0.61, 95% CI 0.43-0.87) and lower incidence of CRS (HR 0.68) and ICANS (HR 0.50) compared to CAR T.

Rilvegostomig — Mechanism: Dual PD-1/TIGIT bispecific antibody — Phase: 2 (I-SPY 2.2) — Population: Immune-positive, high-risk, HER2-negative breast cancer — Comparator: Historical/adaptive control — Results: 53% pCR in immune-positive subgroups; 57% in HR+/immune+, 52% in HR-/immune+.

Izalontamab brengitecan — Mechanism: Bispecific ADC targeting EGFR (high affinity) and HER3 (low affinity) with topoisomerase I inhibitor payload — Phase: 3 (PANKU-Breast02) — Population: Pretreated metastatic TNBC (1-2 prior lines) — Comparator: Physician's choice chemo (eribulin, capecitabine, etc.) — Results: Median PFS 8.5 vs 3.1 mo (HR 0.29); Median OS 15.9 vs 12.5 mo (HR 0.60).

Ivonescimab — Mechanism: PD-1/VEGF dual-target bispecific antibody — Phase: 3 (HARMONi-6) — Population: Previously untreated advanced squamous NSCLC — Comparator: Tislelizumab + chemotherapy — Results: Median OS 27.89 vs 23.69 months; HR 0.66; consistent benefit across PD-L1 subgroups (HR 0.64–0.68).

Teclistamab / Talquetamab — Mechanism: BCMAxCD3 / GPRC5DxCD3 bispecific antibodies — Phase: 2 (OPTec/OPTal) — Population: Relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma — Intervention: Prophylactic tocilizumab — Results: 0% grade 3+ CRS or ICANS; ORR 68.9% (teclistamab) and 71.4% (talquetamab); 75.6% progression-free at 11.8 months (teclistamab arm).

Teclistamab — Mechanism: BCMAxCD3 bispecific T-cell engager — Phase: 3 (MajesTEC-9) — Population: RRMM (1-3 prior lines) — Comparator: PVd/Kd — Results: HR 0.29 PFS, HR 0.60 OS; ORR 84.5% vs 54.2%; CR+ 65.9% vs 16.8%.

Ivonescimab — Mechanism: PD-1/VEGF bispecific antibody — Phase: 3 — Population: Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer — Comparator: Chemotherapy + PD-1 inhibitor — Results: 34% reduction in risk of death; validated bispecific efficacy in lung cancer.

Dark Horse Consulting Group / Altruist Biologics — Strategic MOU — Bispecific and ADC manufacturing — Integrated service model combining DHCG regulatory/CMC expertise with Altruist's 232,000L China production capacity.

Dark Horse Consulting Group / Altruist Biologics — Strategic MOU — Bispecific antibody manufacturing — Partnership launched to integrate DHCG's regulatory/CMC consulting with Altruist's China-based cGMP infrastructure for bispecific and ADC programs.
Akeso / Summit Therapeutics — Licensing agreement for ivonescimab — PD-1/VEGF bispecific — Deal value undisclosed; triggered wave of industry investments following Phase 3 success in lung cancer.

Summit Therapeutics / Akeso — Licensing of PD-1/VEGF bispecific — Ivonescimab — Phase 3 success in lung cancer (34% mortality reduction) spurred billions in industry deals and investment in bispecific antibody class.
